body:
### Problem

The #34493 added 'busy' option for buffers and it is a really neat way to indicate that "something is being done". However, I have some problems as to how to actually use it in contexts that I'd expect it to work.

#### Frequent fast background tasks

There are several cases of frequently executed and usually fast background tasks, like LSP servers computing diagnostics. I think it would be more user friendly for them to be able to adjust 'busy' and give a feedback that something is being done. But as they are frequent, it will result in unwanted flickering of any visual feedback for this (like a symbol in default statusline).

#### Editor level busy

There are cases that would benefit from indicating that the entire editor is busy, like if it is blocked by waiting for user input. As in Operator-pending mode or after `vim.fn.getchar()`. Currently 'busy' is only buffer-local, which in theory can be used by adjusting 'busy' *for all* buffers, but it is not quite convenient and not full proof (in theory there could be new buffers created and all of them will have 'busy' 0; `vim.go.busy` has no effect here).

### Expected behavior

This is mostly meant to gather suggestions about how to best approach this. My current ideas are:

1. Add new 'busydelay' option. When more than zero, anything that wants to adjust 'busy' is expected to wait 'busydelay' milliseconds before doing so. Having a default value like 1000 can reduce flickering in cases like LSP servers computing diagnostics (which can then be done in core).
2. Add new 'busyglobal' boolean option to indicate that the editor itself is busy. The visualization of busy state is then expected to check both 'busy' and 'busyglobal'.

I think the 1 is more or less safe and solid solution. Not so sure about 2.

comment_data.body:
> Currently 'busy' is only buffer-local, which in theory can be used by adjusting 'busy' _for all_ buffers

Could make it global-local then set it with `:setglobal` ? 

It currently also is assumed that an ongoing "Progress" message indicates "global busy". And there should be an [ergonomic way](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/22478) for components to check "is there an ongoing progress message"? 



> anything that wants to adjust 'busy' is expected to wait 'busydelay' milliseconds

Can we do this centrally so that plugins don't have to manage this individually? E.g. debounce the statusline.

> Could make it global-local then set it with `:setglobal` ?

As far as I can tell, that would still require adjust the 'busy' value for all already opened buffers. Once when increasing 'busy' and once when decreasing 'busy'.

> It currently also is assumed that an ongoing "Progress" message indicates "global busy". And there should be an [ergonomic way](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/22478) for components to check "is there an ongoing progress message"?

I don't think this fits into applications that I had in mind (like showing that the editor is busy and waits for user input). And it would be relatively noisy.

> Can we do this centrally so that plugins don't have to manage this individually? E.g. debounce the statusline.

Providing an option that can be controlled once and not per plugin is exactly the idea for making this more centralized.

Debouncing default statusline will make it work only in default statusline. For example, I'd want to have a dedicated spinner indicator in my custom statusline.

Maybe instead of "anything that wants to **adjust** 'busy' is expected to wait 'busydelay' milliseconds" the less work globally would be "anything that wants to **show** 'busy' is expected to debounce 'busydelay' milliseconds". As in LSP client should set 'busy' whenever it wants, but statusline/winbar/notify plugins are expected to debounce visualization.

title: More applications of 'busy' option

type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality
type.name: Enhancement
